docs.title: Introduction
docs.text: This site hosts the documentation about the IT tools and their configuration for JACoW conferences.

docs.title: Resources
docs.text:
• JACoW website
  • Indico
  • Indico end-user documentation
  • JACoW Indico plugin

docs.title: 0. Copy the Dotting Script to the correct Location
docs.text:
Place Dotting Script into the Acrobat JavaScript directory. Note that the directory will be named the same as the version of acrobat you are using and may vary among different operating systems. For the latest Acrobat DC on Windows it should be:

C:\Program Files\Adobe\Acrobat DC\Acrobat\Javascripts

docs.title: 1. Revert to classic interface
docs.text: If you have a recent version of Acrobat DC you'll also get the new user interface, which is somehow disorienting and slower for our task. To revert to the classic Acrobat interface, open Acrobat Pro, select the hamburger menu and choose Disable the new Acrobat.

docs.title: 2. Update Acrobat
docs.text: Open the Help menu and select Check for updates. Apply updates if needed and restart Acrobat.

docs.title: 3. Access Preferences Menu
docs.text: Edit > Preferences > General... or use the Ctrl + K shortcut to access the Preference Menu.

docs.title: 4. General settings
docs.text: Stop Acrobat from generating URLs links in distilled papers. (Uncheck “Create links from URLs”):

docs.title: 5. Page display
docs.text: Display fonts using the fonts in the PDF not local fonts. (Uncheck “Use local fonts”):

docs.title: 6. Content Editing
docs.text:
Make sure Adobe Acrobat displays this as they are. (Uncheck “Enable Artificial Bold/Italic Styles”):

While editing if you accidentally type changing text in the PDF you may not notice, by setting the editing text and add text to Wingdings 36 font size it will be obvious immediately:

docs.title: 7. Convert From PDF
docs.text:
Include all fonts during embedding. In the Encapsulated PostScript drop down menu Font inclusion > Embedded and Referenced Fonts, Uncheck Convert TrueType to Type 1:

Setup the PostScript/EPS settings to use the JACoW.joboptions Note: Before do this step must be setup the Adobe Acrobat Distiller DC first.

docs.title: 8. Convert to PDF
docs.text: Setup the PNG Grayscale and Color to High Quality:

docs.title: 8. Identity
docs.text: Place your name in the identity Name field which will be printed on the top of the page when you print a dotted paper:

docs.title: 10. JavaScript
docs.text: JavaScript > JavaScript Security > Check Enable menu items JavaScript execution privileges.

docs.title: 11. Search
docs.text: Configure the Acrobat search tool.

docs.title: 12. Units and Guides
docs.text: Setup the grid lines which will show you the margin rules on the page. These are turned on and off using Ctrl + U:

docs.title: 13 Full Screen
docs.text: Configure the Full Screen to loop through pages:

docs.title: Adobe Acrobat Distiller DC Configuration
docs.text:
This will explain who to add the JACoW-12 options to the distiller etc.    

Now set some preferences according to the screenshots:

docs.title: Default browser && plugins
docs.text:
1. Set Firefox as default browser

  2. Check for updates and install if needed. Restart to apply them.

  3. Install the following plug-ins:

    1. Foxy gestures

    2. uBlock origin

  4. Set your special IPAC instructions page as Firefox's homepage (e.g.: IPAC'25's).

docs.title: Set Acrobat as default PDF application
docs.text:
You should set Acrobat as the PDF viewer to handle PDF files in FF. To switch from from the built-in PDF viewer:

  1. Click the hamburger menu and select Settings.
  2. In the General panel, go down to the Applications section.
  3. Find Portable Document Format (PDF) in the list and click on the entry to select it.
  4. Click on the arrow under the Action column for the above entry and select the User other... from the drop-down menu.
  5. Select Adobe Acrobat and confirm.

docs.title: Font Installation
docs.text:
We need some particular fonts for our editing. In detail:

  1. Barcode font to print the papers at the proceedings office

  2. Embeddable symbol font

  3. Liberation Mono for the URLs and DOIs (Word)

The first two are available in the AcrobatPitStopTools, the latter on Download the fonts from the Free Best Fonts website.

To install them select all font files, right-click and select Install for all users:

docs.title: Update MiKTeX
docs.text:
First of all, update MiKTeX by running this command in your terminal:

    miktex packages update

Important!

This command must be run at least twice (continue until you see no packages updated anymore)

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
MiKTeX is to be installed in User mode (not Admin mode)

Admin Mode leads to package load errors and a mix-up when users are not logged in as Administrators and the flag on “Always” is not set correctly (which requires Admin privs when installing).

Select Settings and configure as per screenshot:

docs.title: TeXstudio macros
docs.text:
TeXstudio is a cross-platform open-source TeX Editor. It has a built-in macro system that uses the QuickJS language. There are some interesting macros in the official repository that can be installed by opening the Menu Macros ⇒ Edit Macros... ⇒ Browse. The scripts under jacow folder are specifically created for our events. Pull them and assign a shortcut to it.

Details on the macros can be found in the IPAC-docs manual.

docs.title: Adobe PDF Printer Configuration
docs.text:
Access the Windows Control Panel > All Control Panel Items > Devices and Printers and Right click the Adobe PDF > Printing Preference.

Select the JACoW-12 default setting...

... page size and other bits and pieces:

docs.title: Enfocus PitStop Activation Process
docs.text:
Tested on PitStop version 21.3.0

Select the menu Help > About Third-Party Plug-Ins > About Enfocus PitStop Pro.

Select Offline Activation

Click Start Trial.

Enter your Enfocus ID then click Start Trial.

Save your Activation Request file to your computer.

Click the link to the offline activation Enfocus webpage. Then Browse to select the Activation Request file on your computer:

Enter your Enfocus ID (e-mail) and password:

Then click Continue to finally activate PitStop.

After having it activated, download the registration file:

Then load the Activation Response file:

Now you can finally celebrate your success:

docs.title: Load Enfocus PitStop JACoW Media Box
docs.text:
In Acrobat, open the Action Lists option from the PitStop Pro menu.

Locate file and click Open.

and your action list is ready for use!

docs.title: Set some important options
docs.text:
After this the editor will be able to attach the JACoW template to a document created with another Word template:

and then select the most recent JACoW Word template available.

docs.title: Enable editing in Word by default
docs.text: Access the menu File > Option > Trust Center > File Block Setting to check Open selected file types in Protected View and allow editing then click OK:

docs.title: PDF comparison tools
docs.text:
The Editor-in-Chief usually needs to iterate through various versions of the final proceedings. A final quality check is needed, task that normally needs to browse through all the papers and pages to spot any possible last-minute issue that could have been introduced by the editing process. Re-doing this visual check for every new version of the proceedings is frustrating and time consuming. Using automatic tools that analyse two PDFs and highlight differences can speed up a lot this task. Acrobat Pro has a very useful function to Compare PDFs, which can also produce a report about the differences. This can be very useful for an editor, however it doesn't scale well with large documents like the final proceedings. It just can't handle those big documents.

Various other tools have been found and some of them tested and used. The following table enlists what can be used by a JACoW Editor-in-Chief, starting from the tools that proved to be more promising for our needs.

Software Platform License Cost (Italy) Notes Tested DiffPDFAlso this LinuxWindows GPL 2 Free Works very well and fast. Tested on full IPAC'25 proceedings.Not updated since 2013, but still works. Yes compare-pdf Linux MIT Free This utility compares PDF files visually by converting each page into images and then comparing them using OpenCV. It is particularly useful for identifying differences between PDF files that may not be apparent through text comparison alone.Option --showdiffs is useful for visual comparison of differences, opens one window at a time for pages with differences. Very fast. Yes diff-pdfhomepage LinuxWindowsMacOS GPL Free CLI-based.Shows differences from two PDFs by over-stamping them and highlight changes with colours Yes i‑net PDFC LinuxWindowsMacOS Proprietary 199 USD/year Looks professional and effective Only online Demo Acrobat Pro WindowsMacOS Proprietary 36,59 €/mo (monthly)24,39 €/mo (annually) Works well but fails on large files (e.g. IPAC'25 final proceedings) Yes PDF-XChange Plus Windows Proprietary 55.00 € No Soda PDF Pro Windows Proprietary 15 €/mo (monthly)7.50 €/mo (annually) You can compare two PDF files in two different ways. One option that you have is to use the Compare Documents feature from the Review Module. This option is recommended because you can compare different versions of the same file, having highlights at every change. So, your files will open up next to each other showing all the differences between one and another. Another option is to use the Side By Side View feature from View Module that allows you to simply open up two files next to each other. Here, the disadvantage here is that for this option are no markings to indicate changes or differences in the document. No Draftable Windows Proprietary $129 USD per user/year
